title: lupinus hirsutus subsp. micranthus
intro: Lupinus hirsutus subsp. micranthus, also known as the hairy lupine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Lupinus. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The hairy lupine has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and relief from respiratory discomfort. Hairy lupine can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a range of preparations to suit individual needs.
